title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NamespaceLifecycle is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NamespaceLifecycle is set
input_row.description: Reject creating objects in a namespace that is undergoing termination.
input_row.rationale: Setting admission control policy to NamespaceLifecycle ensures that objects cannot be created in non-existent namespaces, and that namespaces undergoing termination are not used for creating the new objects. This is recommended to enforce the integrity of the namespace termination process and also for the availability of the newer objects. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --disable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that does not include NamespaceLifecycle.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --disable-admission-plugins parameter to ensure it does not include NamespaceLifecycle. Default Value: By default, NamespaceLifecycle is set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#namespacelifecycle

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_admission_plugin_namespacelifecycle_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to ensure the NamespaceLifecycle admission control plugin is enabled.
- Specifically, check that the --disable-admission-plugins flag does NOT include NamespaceLifecycle.
- This can be automated by:
  1. Retrieving the kube-apiserver Pod specification (typically in the kube-system namespace) using:
     ```
     kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o json
     ```
     or, if running as a static Pod:
     ```
     kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
     ```
  2. Parsing the command/args for the kube-apiserver container to extract the value of --disable-admission-plugins.
  3. Validating that NamespaceLifecycle is NOT present in the --disable-admission-plugins list.
- **Pass Criteria:** NamespaceLifecycle is NOT listed in --disable-admission-plugins (or the flag is absent).
- **Fail Criteria:** NamespaceLifecycle is present in --disable-admission-plugins.
- This check does not require host/OS access and can be performed entirely via Kubernetes APIs and kubectl.
